0.details:
Start by greetings
Tell him him about my new job
Inform him about events and new things in my personal life
Explain to him how my work-life has been
Tell him about an experience that reminded me of a time we had fun
0.model_generation:
Hey there,

I hope you’re doing well. I recently started at Brightside Media as a marketing coordinator, and it’s been an exciting change of pace. The onboarding was intensive, but I’m already contributing ideas to a big social campaign.

On the personal front, I moved into a cozy apartment downtown, adopted a playful rescue pup named Luna, and even enrolled in weekend cooking classes. It’s been a whirlwind adjusting to new routines, but I’m really enjoying it.

Work has been both challenging and rewarding—late nights brainstorming taglines, early-morning coffees, and learning a ton from a supportive team. Every project feels like a chance to grow.

Just yesterday I passed by our old coffee shop and ordered that caramel latte we used to love. It instantly brought back memories of those endless afternoons laughing over board games.

I’d love to hear what’s new with you. Let’s catch up soon.

Best wishes,
Alex
0.final_version:
Hello Ben, 

I hope you're okay and everyone in your family is doing well. I am writing to check up on you as it has been months since we last talked. Today i had an encounter with a client that reminded me of you. The client made a certain joke that reminded me of you and the happy times and memories we shared during our last boys night out. As a result, i just had to check up on you. 

First and foremost, i have so many things to tell you. I don't even know where to start, but i will try and highlight everything briefly. I recently got a new job at a start-up capital firm as a financial advisor. I am very excited to begin this new chapter in my professional career. The orientation and onboarding was somewhat intense and overwhelming but i manage to complete it successfully. My new supervisor was so impressed with my skills, adaptability and work ethic. He has assigned me to the team handling the firm's biggest client. 

With regard to my personal life, I met Terry, a really sweet, kind and beautiful girl. I think she might be the one; i feel so connected and attracted to her. I can't wait to introduce you to her. She is so interesting and fun; she is even teaching me how to prepare and cook meals on weekends. 

With regard to my professional life, it has not been easy adapting to the new workplace and colleagues. Fortunately, i have managed to cope well and navigate workplace dynamics and build professional relationships with colleagues. I am glad the new co-workers have welcomed me to their fold. I am very excited to be working on big projects at the firm. It has also been challenging to achieve a healthy work-life balance but i am doing my best. I am taking it easy each day at a time. I will sure tell you more when we meet in person. 

Please make time, i would really love to hear what's new with you and catch-up. 

Regards, 
Frank.

edits.removed: I hope you’re doing well. I recently started at Brightside Media as a marketing coordinator, and it’s been an exciting change of pace. The onboarding was intensive, but I’m already contributing ideas to a big social campaign.

On the personal front, I moved into a cozy apartment downtown, adopted a playful rescue pup named Luna, and even enrolled in weekend cooking classes. It’s been a whirlwind adjusting to new routines, but I’m really enjoying it.

Work has been both challenging and rewarding—late nights brainstorming taglines, early-morning coffees, and learning a ton from a supportive team. Every project feels like a chance to grow.

Just yesterday I passed by our old coffee shop and ordered that caramel latte we used to love. It instantly brought back memories of those endless afternoons laughing over board games.

I’d love to hear what’s new with you. Let’s catch up soon.

Best wishes,
Alex

1.details:
Start with greetings
Purpose of the message
Indicate that i can relate to his situation: Losing a job
Show empathy and compassion
Offer words of encouragement or reassurance
Offer unconditional support; moral, financial and emotional support
1.model_generation:
Hello,

I’m reaching out to let you know how much I care deeply about you. Losing a job can feel overwhelming, and I want you to know that I truly understand how disheartening this moment can be. You are not alone, and I see the strength you’ve shown in facing this challenge head on. It’s okay to feel disappointed or uncertain, and I want you to feel safe sharing whatever you’re experiencing. I believe in your abilities and resilience; this setback does not define your worth or your future success. Whenever you need someone to talk through ideas, review a resume, or simply lend a listening ear, I am here. I’m fully committed to supporting you in every way possible—morally, financially, and emotionally. You have my unwavering belief and encouragement, and together we will navigate this chapter toward brighter opportunities. Take care of yourself, and remember that I’ve got your back.
1.final_version:
Hello Jack, 

I'm reaching out to let you know that i have your back during this tough times. I know from first hand experience that losing a job is overwhelming, disheartening and stressful, especially during these difficult financial and economic times. I totally understand and relate to what you're going through. Just know that you're not alone, i'm here for you in whichever way. I know you will come out of this stronger than ever, you have demonstrated strength, determination and resilience in even tougher times in the past. I have faith in you bro, you will bounce back within no time. This is just a minor setback, you got what it takes to be successful in your professional life, you've got excellent skills, abilities and competences. I am here for you, whatever you need just name it, be it a job referral or just a chat over drinks or lunch. Take care and remember i've got your back. 

Best of wishes, 
Frank.

edits.removed: ’m reaching out to let you know how much I care deeply about you. Losing a job can feel overwhelming, and I
I want you to know that I truly understand how disheartening this moment can be.
You are not alone, and I see the strength you’ve shown in facing this challenge head on. It’s okay to feel disappointed or uncertain, and I want you to feel safe sharing whatever you’re experiencing.

I believe in your abilities and resilience; this setback does not define your worth or your future success. Whenever you need someone to talk through ideas, review a resume, or simply lend a listening ear, I am here. I’m fully committed to supporting you in every way possible—morally, financially, and emotionally. You have my unwavering belief and encouragement, and together we will navigate this chapter toward brighter opportunities. Take care of yourself, and remember that I’ve got your back.

2.details:
Apology letter to a close family friend; Start with greetings
Acknowledge that i know i did wrong by failing to keep my promise of attending the graduation ceremony: Apologize genuinely
Explain what went wrong and why i didn't make to attend her graduation: I had an emergency at work due to a pushed deadline
Emphasize that i will make amends or make it up to her in a major way
Offer belated congratulatory message.
2.model_generation:
Dear friend,

I hope you’re doing well. I want to sincerely apologize for letting you down by missing your graduation ceremony. I know how important this milestone was, and I feel awful for not standing by my promise to celebrate with you.

On the day of your big moment, an unexpected work emergency forced me to stay late—my team faced a critical deadline I couldn’t postpone. Though I tried to resolve it quickly, I wasn’t able to leave in time to join you. Still, I recognize that this explanation does not excuse my absence.

To make it up to you, I would love to plan a special day—dinner, a small gift, or another way to honor your achievement. You deserve to be celebrated fully, and I’m committed to making amends. Congratulations on your graduation—I’m so proud of you and excited to see what comes next.

With heartfelt apologies and warm wishes,

Sincerely,
Your friend
2.final_version:
Dear friend,

I hope you’re doing well. I want to sincerely apologize for letting you down by missing your graduation ceremony. I know how important this milestone was, and I feel awful for not standing by my promise to celebrate with you. I am genuinely sorry that i didn't make it on your big day; i know it meant a big deal to you. 

On the day of your big moment, an unexpected work emergency forced me to stay late—my team faced a critical deadline I couldn’t postpone. Though I tried to resolve it quickly, I wasn’t able to leave in time to join you. Still, I recognize that this explanation does not excuse my absence.

To make it up to you, I would love to plan a special day—dinner, a small gift, or another way to honor your achievement. You deserve to be celebrated fully, and I’m committed to making amends. Congratulations on your graduation—I’m so proud of you and excited to see what comes next. 

With heartfelt apologies and warm wishes,

Sincerely,
Frank.

3.details:
Letter of condolence to a female friend who recently lost her dad
Start with a hello and offer genuine and heartfelt condolences
Convery empathy, emotional support and compassion while recognizing the grief and loss associated with losing a loved one, in this case a parent
Include several happy memories of the deceased
Clearly communicate that i'm there for her during this difficult time as she processes and deals with grief
3.model_generation:
Hello,

I was deeply saddened to hear about your dad’s passing. Please accept my heartfelt condolences during this difficult time. Your father was such a warm and caring presence, and I will always remember the afternoons we spent fishing at the lake, listening to his encouraging stories and laughter echoing across the water. I can still picture him tending the rose garden, proudly showing us the first bloom each season. Sunday brunches at your family home, where he shared his secret pancake recipe, remain some of my fondest memories.

I know how heavy the weight of grief can feel, and I want you to know that I am here for you—whether you need someone to talk with, a shoulder to cry on, or simply a quiet companion. As you navigate the days ahead, know that it’s okay to take all the time you need to grieve and heal. You are not alone. Please lean on me whenever you need support.

With all my love and sympathy,
3.final_version:
Hi Daisy,

I was deeply saddened to hear about your dad’s passing. Please accept my heartfelt condolences during this difficult time. Your father was such a warm and caring presence, and I will always remember the afternoons we spent fishing at the lake, listening to his encouraging stories and laughter echoing across the water. I can still picture him tending the rose garden, proudly showing us the first bloom each season. Sunday brunches at your family home, where he shared his secret pancake recipe, remain some of my fondest memories. I personally feel the loss; i looked up to him as a father figure, mentor and friend. 

I know how heavy the weight of grief can feel, and I want you to know that I am here for you—whether you need someone to talk with, a shoulder to cry on, or simply a quiet companion. As you navigate the days ahead, know that it’s okay to take all the time you need to grieve and heal. You are not alone. Please lean on me whenever you need support.

With all my love and sympathy,
Frank

4.details:
Writing a speech for my best friend's birthday party
Start with greetings for everyone at the party
Open with a funny joke to lighten the mood before starting the speech
Offer a brief background of how i met him and how we became friends: We met in college during orientation week and we have been friend since
offer wishes and hopes for him as he welcomes a new age
End with a catchy and funny statement about him and propose a toast
4.model_generation:
Hello everyone! Welcome to the celebration of the man of the hour! They say wisdom comes with age— in your case, it’s more like a fire hazard with all those candles!

I first met him back in college orientation week, bonding over leftover pizza and mutual confusion about our dorms. Since then, we’ve been partners in crime, late-night study sessions, and countless laughs. Your loyalty and quick wit have made you not just a friend, but family.

As you turn another year wiser (though we’ll debate the ‘wise’ part), my wish for you is a year of health, laughter, big dreams, and a few unforgettable misadventures. May you find reasons to smile each day and great friends by your side.

So here’s to the birthday boy: still the only guy I know who can trip over air and make it look cool. Raise your glass—cheers to you!
4.final_version:
Hello everyone!

I'm glad to join you all as we celebrate the birthday of my best friend, Ben. I know Ben is nervous of what i might say as i know all his dirty secrets ones i can't even tell his girlfriend... laughs. I remember when i first met Ben during college orientation, we were both lost trying to locate the freshmen hostels. We just connected right away and have been friends since. I wish him all the best as he turns a new age, i hope it comes with great accomplishments, opportunities, blessing and breakthroughs, and not forget more crazy boys nights and adventures. Today is his day so let him enjoy to the fullest. make sure to give him whatever he wants, regardless of the ask. I know you all got fun memories with Ben, we all know he loves fun, new adventures and anything involving bad decisions. Help me wish Ben the Happiest Birthday ever, raise a glass for him! Cheers!!

edits.removed: Here is to Ben, raise a glass for this great gentleman!

5.details:
Thank you letter to a friend who helped me to relocate and find a new house
Start with warm greetings
Highlight what he did and why his actions meant so much to me, including the impact of his actions
Recognize and appreciate his efforts
Include that you will make it up to him, for example with a nice treat
Promise to be there when he needs you
5.model_generation:
Dear John,

I hope this note finds you well. I wanted to express my heartfelt gratitude for all the help you gave me during my relocation. Packing boxes, scouting neighborhoods, and guiding me through each step of finding the perfect home went far beyond what I could have asked for.

Your generosity and unwavering support meant the world to me. Thanks to your tireless efforts—driving me to view houses, sharing your insider tips, and even lending a hand on moving day—I feel settled and confident in my new surroundings. Your kindness eased my stress and turned a daunting process into an adventure I’ll always remember.

To show my appreciation, I’d love to treat you to dinner or surprise you with your favorite dessert. Please know I’m here for you too, whenever you need a hand or just someone to lean on.

Thank you again for everything. Your friendship is a gift.

Sincerely,
Michael
5.final_version:
Hello Ben, 

I am writing to thank you for helping me to relocate and find a new house. I am grateful and i really appreciate what you did for me. I don't take it for granted that you made time out of your busy schedule and help me. I am overwhelmed with gratitude my heart is full, you're the best best friend in the whole world and some parts of heaven. You made it easy for me to relocate and settle in as you know the stress that comes with moving and finding a new house. Thanks to you i didn't experience serious stress and challenges that come with relocation and house hunting. To show my appreciation, i have planned a surprise treat where you will get to enjoy the finest things and experiences from a hidden gem that i recently discovered. It will be a treat that you'll never forget, you'll tell stories about it in years to come. I promise to be there when you need me. I'm just a call a way bro, I'm forever grateful and indebted to you. THANK YOU!!

Sincerely. 
Frank

post_survey.feedback: I enjoyed taking part in the study. I welcome an opportunity to participate in future studies.
